title: Adolescents CBT Core Series
description: A 30-page English CBT core workbook for adolescents, organized into six stages that help teens understand what is happening, notice body signals and thoughts, check evidence, choose helpful actions, work with avoidance, and continue weekly practice.
therapeuticGoal: Support adolescents in structured CBT self-practice across emotions, automatic thoughts, evidence-checking, helpful action planning, behavior activation, and weekly continuity.
whenToUse: Use when a teen asks for a CBT workbook, structured CBT forms, anxiety/stress thought-work, avoidance support, or a weekly check-in coping plan in English.

en.title: Adolescents CBT Core Series
en.description: A 30-page English CBT core workbook for adolescents, organized into six stages that help teens understand what is happening, notice body signals and thoughts, check evidence, choose helpful actions, work with avoidance, and continue weekly practice.

therapeutic_goal: Support adolescents in structured CBT self-practice across emotions, automatic thoughts, evidence-checking, helpful action planning, behavior activation, and weekly continuity.
when_to_use: Use when a teen asks for a CBT workbook, structured CBT forms, anxiety/stress thought-work, avoidance support, or a weekly check-in coping plan in English.

title: What Is Going On for Me Right Now?
description: Recognize your current state and begin identifying what you are experiencing right now.
therapeuticGoal: Build first-step awareness of present thoughts, feelings, and context.
whenToUse: Use when a teen needs a starting point to pause and describe what is happening.

en.title: What Is Going On for Me Right Now?
en.description: Recognize your current state and begin identifying what you are experiencing right now.

therapeutic_goal: Build first-step awareness of present thoughts, feelings, and context.
when_to_use: Use when a teen needs a starting point to pause and describe what is happening.

title: My Body Gives Me Signals
description: Identify body cues linked to stress, anxiety, and emotional activation.
therapeuticGoal: Help teens notice body sensations as early warning and regulation signals.
whenToUse: Use for body-signal, physical symptom, or emotion-in-the-body requests.

en.title: My Body Gives Me Signals
en.description: Identify body cues linked to stress, anxiety, and emotional activation.

therapeutic_goal: Help teens notice body sensations as early warning and regulation signals.
when_to_use: Use for body-signal, physical symptom, or emotion-in-the-body requests.

title: What Triggered Me?
description: Identify triggering situations, places, thoughts, or comments.
therapeuticGoal: Increase trigger awareness before reaction patterns escalate.
whenToUse: Use for trigger-identification and what-started-this requests.

en.title: What Triggered Me?
en.description: Identify triggering situations, places, thoughts, or comments.

therapeutic_goal: Increase trigger awareness before reaction patterns escalate.
when_to_use: Use for trigger-identification and what-started-this requests.

title: Thought, Feeling, Action
description: Map the relationship between thought, emotion, and behavior.
therapeuticGoal: Teach the core CBT triangle connection in practical terms.
whenToUse: Use when the user asks for thought-feeling-action mapping.

en.title: Thought, Feeling, Action
en.description: Map the relationship between thought, emotion, and behavior.

therapeutic_goal: Teach the core CBT triangle connection in practical terms.
when_to_use: Use when the user asks for thought-feeling-action mapping.

title: My Personal Map
description: Build a personal coping map of what helps and who can support.
therapeuticGoal: Strengthen personal coping structure and support planning.
whenToUse: Use when a teen asks to map supports and helpful actions.

en.title: My Personal Map
en.description: Build a personal coping map of what helps and who can support.

therapeutic_goal: Strengthen personal coping structure and support planning.
when_to_use: Use when a teen asks to map supports and helpful actions.

title: What Did My Mind Say?
description: Capture first automatic thoughts after a stressful situation.
therapeuticGoal: Increase awareness of quick automatic thought content.
whenToUse: Use for automatic-thought identification requests.

en.title: What Did My Mind Say?
en.description: Capture first automatic thoughts after a stressful situation.

therapeutic_goal: Increase awareness of quick automatic thought content.
when_to_use: Use for automatic-thought identification requests.

title: Thought or Fact?
description: Differentiate interpretations from objective facts.
therapeuticGoal: Improve cognitive differentiation and reduce fusion with thoughts.
whenToUse: Use for thought-versus-fact clarification requests.

en.title: Thought or Fact?
en.description: Differentiate interpretations from objective facts.

therapeutic_goal: Improve cognitive differentiation and reduce fusion with thoughts.
when_to_use: Use for thought-versus-fact clarification requests.

title: Which Interpretation Did I Give It?
description: Review multiple interpretations of one event.
therapeuticGoal: Expand interpretation flexibility and reduce single-story thinking.
whenToUse: Use when users ask about interpretation bias or alternative interpretations.

en.title: Which Interpretation Did I Give It?
en.description: Review multiple interpretations of one event.

therapeutic_goal: Expand interpretation flexibility and reduce single-story thinking.
when_to_use: Use when users ask about interpretation bias or alternative interpretations.

title: What Kind of Thought Is This?
description: Identify common distorted thinking styles or patterns.
therapeuticGoal: Help teens label thought patterns that intensify distress.
whenToUse: Use for thinking-pattern classification and distortion requests.

en.title: What Kind of Thought Is This?
en.description: Identify common distorted thinking styles or patterns.

therapeutic_goal: Help teens label thought patterns that intensify distress.
when_to_use: Use for thinking-pattern classification and distortion requests.

title: What Belief Lies Underneath?
description: Explore deeper beliefs beneath repeated automatic thoughts.
therapeuticGoal: Surface core belief themes to guide restructuring work.
whenToUse: Use for deeper-belief or underlying-message requests.

en.title: What Belief Lies Underneath?
en.description: Explore deeper beliefs beneath repeated automatic thoughts.

therapeutic_goal: Surface core belief themes to guide restructuring work.
when_to_use: Use for deeper-belief or underlying-message requests.

title: What Is the Evidence?
description: List evidence for and against a thought.
therapeuticGoal: Support evidence-based thought checking and balanced evaluation.
whenToUse: Use for evidence-for-against thought requests.

en.title: What Is the Evidence?
en.description: List evidence for and against a thought.

therapeutic_goal: Support evidence-based thought checking and balanced evaluation.
when_to_use: Use for evidence-for-against thought requests.

title: Is There Another Way to See This?
description: Practice generating alternative perspectives.
therapeuticGoal: Increase cognitive flexibility and reduce rigid interpretations.
whenToUse: Use for reframing and alternative-view requests.

en.title: Is There Another Way to See This?
en.description: Practice generating alternative perspectives.

therapeutic_goal: Increase cognitive flexibility and reduce rigid interpretations.
when_to_use: Use for reframing and alternative-view requests.

title: What Would I Say to a Friend?
description: Use compassionate external perspective to support self-talk.
therapeuticGoal: Develop kinder, more supportive internal dialogue.
whenToUse: Use for self-compassion and friend-perspective prompts.

en.title: What Would I Say to a Friend?
en.description: Use compassionate external perspective to support self-talk.

therapeutic_goal: Develop kinder, more supportive internal dialogue.
when_to_use: Use for self-compassion and friend-perspective prompts.

title: A More Balanced Thought
description: Build a balanced thought that includes both difficulty and reality.
therapeuticGoal: Translate thought checking into practical balanced thinking.
whenToUse: Use when a user asks to create a balanced thought.

en.title: A More Balanced Thought
en.description: Build a balanced thought that includes both difficulty and reality.

therapeutic_goal: Translate thought checking into practical balanced thinking.
when_to_use: Use when a user asks to create a balanced thought.

title: What Do I Choose to Think Now?
description: Choose a more helpful thought after reviewing evidence.
therapeuticGoal: Support intentional thought selection after cognitive review.
whenToUse: Use when moving from analysis to chosen replacement thought.

en.title: What Do I Choose to Think Now?
en.description: Choose a more helpful thought after reviewing evidence.

therapeutic_goal: Support intentional thought selection after cognitive review.
when_to_use: Use when moving from analysis to chosen replacement thought.

title: Choosing an Action
description: Select one or more actions that fit the current situation.
therapeuticGoal: Bridge CBT insights into concrete action choices.
whenToUse: Use for action-selection and next-step requests.

en.title: Choosing an Action
en.description: Select one or more actions that fit the current situation.

therapeutic_goal: Bridge CBT insights into concrete action choices.
when_to_use: Use for action-selection and next-step requests.

title: Creating a Helpful Thought
description: Construct practical helpful thoughts using structure.
therapeuticGoal: Improve quality and usefulness of coping self-statements.
whenToUse: Use for helpful-thought formulation requests.

en.title: Creating a Helpful Thought
en.description: Construct practical helpful thoughts using structure.

therapeutic_goal: Improve quality and usefulness of coping self-statements.
when_to_use: Use for helpful-thought formulation requests.

title: Small Steps
description: Break a challenge into manageable action steps.
therapeuticGoal: Increase behavioral momentum through stepwise planning.
whenToUse: Use for task breakdown and gradual-start planning.

en.title: Small Steps
en.description: Break a challenge into manageable action steps.

therapeutic_goal: Increase behavioral momentum through stepwise planning.
when_to_use: Use for task breakdown and gradual-start planning.

title: Thinking, Beliefs and Assumptions
description: Examine assumptions connected to recurring thoughts.
therapeuticGoal: Make hidden assumptions explicit and reviewable.
whenToUse: Use for assumptions or beliefs-behind-thoughts requests.

en.title: Thinking, Beliefs and Assumptions
en.description: Examine assumptions connected to recurring thoughts.

therapeutic_goal: Make hidden assumptions explicit and reviewable.
when_to_use: Use for assumptions or beliefs-behind-thoughts requests.

title: Balance and Evaluation
description: Evaluate thought strength, evidence balance, and adjustment needs.
therapeuticGoal: Support reflective evaluation of current thought patterns.
whenToUse: Use for balancing and evaluating current beliefs.

en.title: Balance and Evaluation
en.description: Evaluate thought strength, evidence balance, and adjustment needs.

therapeutic_goal: Support reflective evaluation of current thought patterns.
when_to_use: Use for balancing and evaluating current beliefs.

title: Avoidance
description: Identify what is being avoided and the cost of avoidance.
therapeuticGoal: Increase awareness of avoidance cycles and consequences.
whenToUse: Use for avoidance-focused requests.

en.title: Avoidance
en.description: Identify what is being avoided and the cost of avoidance.

therapeutic_goal: Increase awareness of avoidance cycles and consequences.
when_to_use: Use for avoidance-focused requests.

title: Small Steps
description: Plan immediate small behavioral steps forward.
therapeuticGoal: Enable action despite fear using tiny doable steps.
whenToUse: Use when user asks for small-step behavior change.

en.title: Small Steps
en.description: Plan immediate small behavioral steps forward.

therapeutic_goal: Enable action despite fear using tiny doable steps.
when_to_use: Use when user asks for small-step behavior change.

title: Gradual Exposure
description: Build a graded exposure ladder from easier to harder steps.
therapeuticGoal: Support safe progressive approach to feared situations.
whenToUse: Use for graded exposure or fear-ladder requests.

en.title: Gradual Exposure
en.description: Build a graded exposure ladder from easier to harder steps.

therapeutic_goal: Support safe progressive approach to feared situations.
when_to_use: Use for graded exposure or fear-ladder requests.

title: Effective Action
description: Choose actions that create meaningful forward movement.
therapeuticGoal: Strengthen effective coping behavior choices.
whenToUse: Use for practical action effectiveness requests.

en.title: Effective Action
en.description: Choose actions that create meaningful forward movement.

therapeutic_goal: Strengthen effective coping behavior choices.
when_to_use: Use for practical action effectiveness requests.

title: Persistence and Tracking
description: Track practice over time to build persistence.
therapeuticGoal: Support follow-through and progress monitoring.
whenToUse: Use for tracking progress and staying consistent.

en.title: Persistence and Tracking
en.description: Track practice over time to build persistence.

therapeutic_goal: Support follow-through and progress monitoring.
when_to_use: Use for tracking progress and staying consistent.

title: What Have I Learned About Myself?
description: Reflect on key learning from recent CBT practice.
therapeuticGoal: Consolidate learning and reinforce self-understanding.
whenToUse: Use at reflection points or completion of a workbook cycle.

en.title: What Have I Learned About Myself?
en.description: Reflect on key learning from recent CBT practice.

therapeutic_goal: Consolidate learning and reinforce self-understanding.
when_to_use: Use at reflection points or completion of a workbook cycle.

title: My Weekly Check-In
description: Weekly structured review of attempts, wins, barriers, and lessons.
therapeuticGoal: Maintain continuity of CBT practice week to week.
whenToUse: Use for weekly check-in and continuity requests.

en.title: My Weekly Check-In
en.description: Weekly structured review of attempts, wins, barriers, and lessons.

therapeutic_goal: Maintain continuity of CBT practice week to week.
when_to_use: Use for weekly check-in and continuity requests.

title: Strengthening Myself
description: Identify strengths and self-encouragement that support progress.
therapeuticGoal: Reinforce positive self-recognition and resilience.
whenToUse: Use for confidence-building and strength reflection.

en.title: Strengthening Myself
en.description: Identify strengths and self-encouragement that support progress.

therapeutic_goal: Reinforce positive self-recognition and resilience.
when_to_use: Use for confidence-building and strength reflection.

title: When It’s Hard for Me - What Helps Me?
description: Plan supports and responses for difficult moments.
therapeuticGoal: Create proactive coping plans for high-stress periods.
whenToUse: Use for relapse-prevention and hard-moment planning.

en.title: When It’s Hard for Me - What Helps Me?
en.description: Plan supports and responses for difficult moments.

therapeutic_goal: Create proactive coping plans for high-stress periods.
when_to_use: Use for relapse-prevention and hard-moment planning.

title: My Road Card
description: Summarize personal goals and key coping steps in one reference card.
therapeuticGoal: Provide a concise go-forward personal CBT roadmap.
whenToUse: Use when creating a final summary plan to revisit later.

en.title: My Road Card
en.description: Summarize personal goals and key coping steps in one reference card.

therapeutic_goal: Provide a concise go-forward personal CBT roadmap.
when_to_use: Use when creating a final summary plan to revisit later.

title: Adolescents CBT Specialized Series
description: A full 60-form specialized English CBT workbook for adolescents (modules 01–10).
therapeuticGoal: Provide structured, age-appropriate specialized CBT support across common adolescent clinical needs.
whenToUse: Use when a teen or caregiver explicitly asks for the full Adolescents CBT Specialized Series workbook in English.

en.title: Adolescents CBT Specialized Series
en.description: A full 60-form specialized English CBT workbook for adolescents (modules 01–10).

therapeutic_goal: Provide structured, age-appropriate specialized CBT support across common adolescent clinical needs.
when_to_use: Use when a teen or caregiver explicitly asks for the full Adolescents CBT Specialized Series workbook in English.

title: Module 01 — Anxiety, Stress and Fears
description: Calm, practical CBT tools for anxiety, stress, worries, fears, and avoidance with supportive step-by-step coping.
therapeuticGoal: Support anxious teens in noticing stress patterns, reducing avoidance, and building courage through manageable practice.
whenToUse: Use for anxiety, stress overload, worry spirals, fear of situations, and test-stress coping.

en.title: Module 01 — Anxiety, Stress and Fears
en.description: Calm, practical CBT tools for anxiety, stress, worries, fears, and avoidance with supportive step-by-step coping.

therapeutic_goal: Support anxious teens in noticing stress patterns, reducing avoidance, and building courage through manageable practice.
when_to_use: Use for anxiety, stress overload, worry spirals, fear of situations, and test-stress coping.

title: Module 02 — Mood, Functioning and Energy
description: Supportive worksheets for low mood, daily functioning, activation, and energy reset planning.
therapeuticGoal: Help teens improve mood and functioning through structured, realistic activation and support routines.
whenToUse: Use for low mood, low motivation, heavy feelings, low energy, and mood reset support.

en.title: Module 02 — Mood, Functioning and Energy
en.description: Supportive worksheets for low mood, daily functioning, activation, and energy reset planning.

therapeutic_goal: Help teens improve mood and functioning through structured, realistic activation and support routines.
when_to_use: Use for low mood, low motivation, heavy feelings, low energy, and mood reset support.

title: Module 03 — Self-Esteem and Identity
description: CBT forms for self-criticism, identity pressure, values clarity, and steadier self-worth.
therapeuticGoal: Build healthier self-view and values-based identity while reducing self-critical loops.
whenToUse: Use for self-esteem struggles, comparison stress, identity confusion, and values reflection.

en.title: Module 03 — Self-Esteem and Identity
en.description: CBT forms for self-criticism, identity pressure, values clarity, and steadier self-worth.

therapeutic_goal: Build healthier self-view and values-based identity while reducing self-critical loops.
when_to_use: Use for self-esteem struggles, comparison stress, identity confusion, and values reflection.

title: Module 04 — Friendship, Belonging, and Conflict
description: Worksheets for friendship stress, belonging, conflict navigation, and repair-oriented communication.
therapeuticGoal: Strengthen social confidence, boundaries, and conflict repair skills in peer contexts.
whenToUse: Use for friendship worries, exclusion pain, conflict, boundary-setting, and repair conversations.

en.title: Module 04 — Friendship, Belonging, and Conflict
en.description: Worksheets for friendship stress, belonging, conflict navigation, and repair-oriented communication.

therapeutic_goal: Strengthen social confidence, boundaries, and conflict repair skills in peer contexts.
when_to_use: Use for friendship worries, exclusion pain, conflict, boundary-setting, and repair conversations.

title: Module 05 — Anger, Impulsivity, and Regulation
description: Tools for noticing escalation early, pausing impulses, calming down, and repairing after outbursts.
therapeuticGoal: Improve pause-and-respond capacity and support repair after high-intensity reactions.
whenToUse: Use for anger spikes, impulsive reactions, calm-down planning, and repair after blow-ups.

en.title: Module 05 — Anger, Impulsivity, and Regulation
en.description: Tools for noticing escalation early, pausing impulses, calming down, and repairing after outbursts.

therapeutic_goal: Improve pause-and-respond capacity and support repair after high-intensity reactions.
when_to_use: Use for anger spikes, impulsive reactions, calm-down planning, and repair after blow-ups.

title: Module 06 — OCD, Intrusive Thoughts, and New Responses
description: Age-appropriate support for noticing intrusive thoughts and urges while practicing safer new responses without reassurance loops.
therapeuticGoal: Help teens reduce ritual/reassurance cycles by building tolerant, values-guided response practice.
whenToUse: Use for intrusive or sticky thoughts, urges, rituals, reassurance patterns, and practicing new responses.

en.title: Module 06 — OCD, Intrusive Thoughts, and New Responses
en.description: Age-appropriate support for noticing intrusive thoughts and urges while practicing safer new responses without reassurance loops.

therapeutic_goal: Help teens reduce ritual/reassurance cycles by building tolerant, values-guided response practice.
when_to_use: Use for intrusive or sticky thoughts, urges, rituals, reassurance patterns, and practicing new responses.

title: Module 07 — ADHD, Attention, Organization, and Impulsivity
description: Practical CBT tools for attention, task-start friction, planning, organization, and impulse control.
therapeuticGoal: Increase functional follow-through with clear task scaffolds, focus supports, and regulation habits.
whenToUse: Use for focus struggles, getting started, task steps, distraction, organization, and impulsivity.

en.title: Module 07 — ADHD, Attention, Organization, and Impulsivity
en.description: Practical CBT tools for attention, task-start friction, planning, organization, and impulse control.

therapeutic_goal: Increase functional follow-through with clear task scaffolds, focus supports, and regulation habits.
when_to_use: Use for focus struggles, getting started, task steps, distraction, organization, and impulsivity.

title: Module 08 — Body, Sleep, and Stress
description: Regulation worksheets for body stress, sleep routines, breathing, and overload-recovery balance.
therapeuticGoal: Strengthen body-based regulation and healthier sleep-stress balance for day-to-day resilience.
whenToUse: Use for body stress, sleep issues, breathing support, overload, body check-ins, and balance plans.

en.title: Module 08 — Body, Sleep, and Stress
en.description: Regulation worksheets for body stress, sleep routines, breathing, and overload-recovery balance.

therapeutic_goal: Strengthen body-based regulation and healthier sleep-stress balance for day-to-day resilience.
when_to_use: Use for body stress, sleep issues, breathing support, overload, body check-ins, and balance plans.

title: Module 09 — Trauma and Safe Coping
description: Gentle, non-graphic trauma-safe coping tools focused on grounding, support, and gradual return.
therapeuticGoal: Promote safety and stabilization through grounding, support mapping, and paced re-engagement.
whenToUse: Use for trauma-safe coping, grounding, safety anchors, support circles, calm-back, and gentle return.

en.title: Module 09 — Trauma and Safe Coping
en.description: Gentle, non-graphic trauma-safe coping tools focused on grounding, support, and gradual return.

therapeutic_goal: Promote safety and stabilization through grounding, support mapping, and paced re-engagement.
when_to_use: Use for trauma-safe coping, grounding, safety anchors, support circles, calm-back, and gentle return.

title: Module 10 — Parents and Teens
description: Family communication worksheets for parents and teens: listening, trust, autonomy, and repair-focused conversation.
therapeuticGoal: Support healthier parent-teen communication, shared understanding, and collaborative independence-building.
whenToUse: Use for home communication stress, parent understanding, listening, independence, trust, and family conversation.

en.title: Module 10 — Parents and Teens
en.description: Family communication worksheets for parents and teens: listening, trust, autonomy, and repair-focused conversation.

therapeutic_goal: Support healthier parent-teen communication, shared understanding, and collaborative independence-building.
when_to_use: Use for home communication stress, parent understanding, listening, independence, trust, and family conversation.

title: What Am I Feeling?
description: Identify and name current feelings using child-friendly choices.
therapeuticGoal: Identify and name current feelings using child-friendly choices.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs help noticing and naming feelings.
aiMatchingSummary: Notice the main feeling, where it shows up, and one thing happening right now.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: What Am I Feeling?
en.description: Identify and name current feelings using child-friendly choices.

therapeutic_goal: Identify and name current feelings using child-friendly choices.
when_to_use: Use when a child needs help noticing and naming feelings.
ai_matching_summary: Notice the main feeling, where it shows up, and one thing happening right now.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: What Is My Body Telling Me?
description: Connect body clues to emotions and internal states.
therapeuticGoal: Connect body clues to emotions and internal states.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs help noticing body signals linked to emotions.
aiMatchingSummary: Identify body signals, body locations, and one strong clue the child notices.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: What Is My Body Telling Me?
en.description: Connect body clues to emotions and internal states.

therapeutic_goal: Connect body clues to emotions and internal states.
when_to_use: Use when a child needs help noticing body signals linked to emotions.
ai_matching_summary: Identify body signals, body locations, and one strong clue the child notices.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: My Feeling Meter
description: Rate feeling intensity and choose matching supports.
therapeuticGoal: Rate feeling intensity and choose matching supports.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs to show how big a feeling is right now.
aiMatchingSummary: Use a 1–5 ladder to rate the size of the feeling and choose what may help.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: My Feeling Meter
en.description: Rate feeling intensity and choose matching supports.

therapeutic_goal: Rate feeling intensity and choose matching supports.
when_to_use: Use when a child needs to show how big a feeling is right now.
ai_matching_summary: Use a 1–5 ladder to rate the size of the feeling and choose what may help.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: Early Signs Detective
description: Notice early warning signs before feelings grow bigger.
therapeuticGoal: Notice early warning signs before feelings grow bigger.
whenToUse: Use when a child is learning to recognize early clues in body or behavior.
aiMatchingSummary: Circle early clues, what may be happening, and note the first clue noticed today.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: Early Signs Detective
en.description: Notice early warning signs before feelings grow bigger.

therapeutic_goal: Notice early warning signs before feelings grow bigger.
when_to_use: Use when a child is learning to recognize early clues in body or behavior.
ai_matching_summary: Circle early clues, what may be happening, and note the first clue noticed today.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: Feelings Can Change
description: Teach that feelings can shift with time and support.
therapeuticGoal: Teach that feelings can shift with time and support.
whenToUse: Use after a hard feeling begins to settle or when teaching hope and flexibility.
aiMatchingSummary: See how feelings change from first to now to after some help, and what helped a little.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: Feelings Can Change
en.description: Teach that feelings can shift with time and support.

therapeutic_goal: Teach that feelings can shift with time and support.
when_to_use: Use after a hard feeling begins to settle or when teaching hope and flexibility.
ai_matching_summary: See how feelings change from first to now to after some help, and what helped a little.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: My Feeling Card
description: Create a simple personal card linking feelings, body clues, supports, and kind words.
therapeuticGoal: Create a simple personal card linking feelings, body clues, supports, and kind words.
whenToUse: Use as a quick take-away tool for daily emotional check-ins.
aiMatchingSummary: Choose the feeling, body clues, helpful tools, and a kind phrase to remember.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: My Feeling Card
en.description: Create a simple personal card linking feelings, body clues, supports, and kind words.

therapeutic_goal: Create a simple personal card linking feelings, body clues, supports, and kind words.
when_to_use: Use as a quick take-away tool for daily emotional check-ins.
ai_matching_summary: Choose the feeling, body clues, helpful tools, and a kind phrase to remember.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: What Is My Thought Saying?
description: Notice a thought as words in the mind and choose a kinder thought.
therapeuticGoal: Help the child notice a thought as words in the mind and choose a kinder thought.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs help naming a thought and noticing whether it feels helpful.
aiMatchingSummary: The child chooses a possible thought, notices how it feels, checks whether it is helpful, and selects a kinder thought.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: What Is My Thought Saying?
en.description: Notice a thought as words in the mind and choose a kinder thought.

therapeutic_goal: Help the child notice a thought as words in the mind and choose a kinder thought.
when_to_use: Use when a child needs help naming a thought and noticing whether it feels helpful.
ai_matching_summary: The child chooses a possible thought, notices how it feels, checks whether it is helpful, and selects a kinder thought.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: Thought or Fact?
description: Separate what happened from what the mind is guessing.
therapeuticGoal: Teach the child to separate what happened from what the mind is guessing.
whenToUse: Use when a child mixes facts with guesses or worries about what others think.
aiMatchingSummary: The worksheet helps distinguish real events from guesses, then choose a more balanced idea.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: Thought or Fact?
en.description: Separate what happened from what the mind is guessing.

therapeutic_goal: Teach the child to separate what happened from what the mind is guessing.
when_to_use: Use when a child mixes facts with guesses or worries about what others think.
ai_matching_summary: The worksheet helps distinguish real events from guesses, then choose a more balanced idea.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: Worry Thoughts
description: Notice worry thoughts kindly and choose simple coping supports.
therapeuticGoal: Help the child notice worry thoughts kindly and choose simple coping supports.
whenToUse: Use when a child has worry thoughts about mistakes, peers, or trying something hard.
aiMatchingSummary: The child identifies what worry says, how worry feels, and what helps when worry appears.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: Worry Thoughts
en.description: Notice worry thoughts kindly and choose simple coping supports.

therapeutic_goal: Help the child notice worry thoughts kindly and choose simple coping supports.
when_to_use: Use when a child has worry thoughts about mistakes, peers, or trying something hard.
ai_matching_summary: The child identifies what worry says, how worry feels, and what helps when worry appears.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: Helpful Thought Switch
description: Practice changing an unhelpful thought into a kinder and more useful thought.
therapeuticGoal: Practice changing an unhelpful thought into a kinder and more useful thought.
whenToUse: Use when a child is stuck in an unhelpful thought such as "I cannot do this" or "I will mess up".
aiMatchingSummary: The child matches unhelpful thoughts with kinder replacements and fills in a personal thought switch.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: Helpful Thought Switch
en.description: Practice changing an unhelpful thought into a kinder and more useful thought.

therapeutic_goal: Practice changing an unhelpful thought into a kinder and more useful thought.
when_to_use: Use when a child is stuck in an unhelpful thought such as "I cannot do this" or "I will mess up".
ai_matching_summary: The child matches unhelpful thoughts with kinder replacements and fills in a personal thought switch.
safety_notes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: My Thought Detective
description: Look for clues before believing every thought.
therapeuticGoal: Encourage the child to look for clues before believing every thought.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs to check assumptions and identify what they know for sure.
aiMatchingSummary: The child names a thought, identifies facts and guesses, and chooses what else could be true.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: My Thought Detective
en.description: Look for clues before believing every thought.

title: A Thought That Helps Me
description: Build a helpful thought the child can use to move forward.
therapeuticGoal: Build a helpful thought the child can use to move forward.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs a simple replacement thought before taking a next step.
aiMatchingSummary: The child notices a feeling, chooses a thought that helps, selects what supports the next action, and writes a personal helpful thought.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: A Thought That Helps Me
en.description: Build a helpful thought the child can use to move forward.

therapeutic_goal: Build a helpful thought the child can use to move forward.

title: What Do I Do When It Feels Hard?
description: Notice first reactions when something feels hard and choose a helpful next step.
therapeuticGoal: Help the child notice first reactions when something feels hard and choose a helpful next step.
whenToUse: Use when a child gets stuck, avoids, gets upset, or needs help choosing a small response.
aiMatchingSummary: Notices what the child does first when something feels hard, what helps most, and one next small choice.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: What Do I Do When It Feels Hard?
en.description: Notice first reactions when something feels hard and choose a helpful next step.

title: My Tiny Brave Step
description: Choose one very small brave step for today.
therapeuticGoal: Help the child choose one very small brave step for today.
whenToUse: Use when a child is avoiding or feeling unsure about starting a task or situation.
aiMatchingSummary: Helps choose what feels hard, one tiny brave step, how big it feels, and a step to try today.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: My Tiny Brave Step
en.description: Choose one very small brave step for today.

title: My Brave Ladder
description: Build a simple graded ladder for brave practice.
therapeuticGoal: Build a simple graded ladder for brave practice.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs a step-by-step path instead of doing everything at once.
aiMatchingSummary: Uses a 1-5 brave ladder, identifies what is being practiced, who can help, and one ladder goal.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: My Brave Ladder
en.description: Build a simple graded ladder for brave practice.

title: Before I Try
description: Prepare the child before taking a small step by identifying supports and readiness tools.
therapeuticGoal: Prepare the child before taking a small step by identifying supports and readiness tools.
whenToUse: Use before a child attempts a new, hard, or avoided action.
aiMatchingSummary: Identifies what feels hard, what helps the child get ready, who can help, and the first step.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

en.title: Before I Try

title: After I Tried
description: Reflect gently after trying and reinforce learning from effort.
therapeuticGoal: Reflect gently after trying and reinforce learning from effort.
whenToUse: Use after the child attempts a small brave step or helpful action.
aiMatchingSummary: Reviews what the child tried, how it went, what helped, and one thing learned.
safetyNotes: Child-safe CBT worksheet. No graphic, frightening, self-harm, or trauma content.

title: My Small Step Plan
description: Create a short plan for the next brave step.
therapeuticGoal: Create a short plan for the next brave step.
whenToUse: Use when a child needs a simple action plan with goal, step, support, and timing.
aiMatchingSummary: Creates a plan with goal, small step, helper, timing, and one brave step pathway.

title: Pause Button
description: Help children pause when they feel upset or overwhelmed.
therapeuticGoal: Help children pause when they feel upset or overwhelmed.

aiMatchingSummary: Use this when the child needs to stop, take a breath, and choose a calmer next step.
